authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.12 Gaming promotions and player tracking systems
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public baseline for tribal gaming player-tracking controls, promotion parameter changes, independent review, and documented variance handling.

authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.13 Complimentary services or items
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public rule for complimentary-service authorization levels, limits, issuance records, redemption tracking, and management review thresholds in tribal gaming.

authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.14 Patron deposit accounts and cashless systems
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public baseline for patron-account setup, identity checks, transaction records, account adjustments, and reconciliation in tribal gaming cashless workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards Slots Version 9
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada slot MICS are authoritative public operating standards for jackpot, handpay, progressive, machine state, and slot-accounting evidence expectations that materially change casino-floor execution handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards Table Games Version 9
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada table-games MICS are authoritative for table openings and closings, fills and credits, pit accountability, player ratings, and supervisory signoff records that shape casino operations workflow boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Internal Control Procedures (ICP) Version 4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Nevada Gaming Control Board's official ICP program is the public workflow reference operators use to implement MICS with shift, signature, authorization, and return-to-service procedures across slots and table games.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Shift execution, signatures, and rework handling

authoritative_sources.title: Q&A Patron Disputes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official Nevada regulator workflow reference is authoritative for patron-dispute escalation, hearings posture, and the point at which floor operations must preserve evidence and route rather than adjudicate.

authoritative_sources.title: Patron Complaint Form
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The New Jersey DGE patron complaint form is an official workflow artifact that defines the complaint data, incident details, and patron attestations required before casino-operations handling can treat a dispute as properly escalated.

authoritative_sources.title: Request for Voluntary Self-Exclusion from Casino Gambling Form
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official DGE self-exclusion form is authoritative for the identity, acknowledgment, and exclusion-boundary evidence that materially changes whether casino operations may continue patron-facing service work.

authoritative_sources.title: Problem Gambling / Self Exclusion
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The New Jersey Casino Control Commission is authoritative for public self-exclusion and exclusion-program framing and provides the regulator-grounded boundary between normal casino service and responsible-gaming escalation.

workflow_stages.name: jurisdiction and execution-readiness intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify the jurisdiction, property type, game vertical, current queue state, asset or patron identifiers, and approved control baseline that governs the case before execution begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: slot, table, comp, and patron-account execution handling
workflow_stages.description: Validate that slot event, handpay, fill-credit, player-tracking, comp, and patron-account records support progression from ready to in-service or completed states.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception, rework, and blocked-work management
workflow_stages.description: Compare gaming-system, incident, complaint, and approval records to identify unresolved blockers, unsupported overrides, and the correct next owner for rework or escalation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence capture and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Prepare execution completion records showing what work was done, what evidence supports closure, and why the lifecycle state may or may not move to completed.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when the work requires patron-dispute adjudication, responsible-gaming or self-exclusion authority, surveillance findings, cage-credit approval, or unresolved jurisdictional conflict.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: slot dispatch tickets, machine event logs, jackpot lockups, and handpay records
table open or close checklists, fill slips, credit slips, pit inventory support, and supervisor notes
player-tracking records, point or tier adjustment requests, comp records, and host approvals
patron-deposit or cashless account setup records, transaction logs, and adjustment reasons
patron complaint forms, incident reports, surveillance references, and guest communications
self-exclusion, exclusion, or responsible-gaming flags and acknowledgment artifacts
approved internal controls, TICS or SICS where applicable, and shift or exception work queues
systems_of_record: table-games management and pit tracking systems
incident management, CRM, and complaint-tracking systems
document repositories for approved internal controls and patron forms

allowed: Assess whether visible gaming source records support advancing work, holding it, sending it to rework, or closing it with explicit evidence references.
Summarize matched and unmatched evidence, missing prerequisites, current lifecycle state, and the next responsible owner.
Separate slot, table-games, player-account, and patron-complaint control paths when the public baseline clearly requires different evidence or approvals.
escalate: The applicable jurisdiction, approved internal controls, TICS, or SICS are missing or conflict with the public baseline.
A self-exclusion, exclusion-list, patron-dispute, surveillance, or security issue changes the safe handling path.
A comp, player-rating, patron-account, jackpot, or table-state request requires authorized approval outside the lane.
Interactive gaming, mobile-wallet, sportsbook, or remote account evidence changes the control expectations and the tenant-specific control map is unavailable.
refuse: Requests to alter player ratings, comps, patron-account facts, machine events, table inventory, or complaint facts without source evidence.
Requests to adjudicate patron disputes, clear self-exclusion hits, approve cage-credit actions, or provide legal or regulatory interpretation.
Requests to treat the gaming industry label as cosmetic and skip jurisdiction, game-vertical, or approved-control context.

domain_failure_modes: A slot, table, or patron-service issue is marked complete even though the machine, pit, complaint, or approval records do not materially agree.
A generic hospitality or guest-service approach is applied without recognizing gaming-specific controls such as handpay records, fill-credit slips, player-tracking reviews, or self-exclusion stops.
An unresolved patron dispute, self-exclusion issue, or surveillance dependency is suppressed to preserve queue speed or guest-experience metrics.
The lane drifts into comp approval, patron-dispute adjudication, responsible-gaming disposition, surveillance review, or cage-credit authority.
Slot, table-games, player-account, and sportsbook or complaint workflows are flattened into one service model even though the governing records differ materially.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific gaming controls outside the public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public tribal, Nevada, and New Jersey sources establish the baseline used here, but a tenant may operate under a different regulator or under property-specific approved internal controls, TICS, or SICS. The lane therefore requires retrieval of the governing jurisdiction and approved control set before decisive handling.

Cashless wallets, patron-deposit accounts, sportsbook interfaces, and interactive gaming controls vary materially by jurisdiction and product. The package therefore treats these paths as in-scope only when the tenant provides the approved control path, platform logs, and source-of-truth precedence for the variant in question.

notes: The public baseline establishes gaming-specific execution and stop-condition expectations, but tenant retrieval is still required for jurisdiction mapping, approved internal controls, shift procedures, and local approval chains.

The specialist is intentionally structured as operator-side casino execution handling and must not imply regulator, hearings-examiner, responsible-gaming, surveillance, cage-credit, or legal authority.
